title: 1.1: Tests that TimeWheel Add() fires the callback with the correct scheduled value when a single callback is scheduled
1.2: Tests that TimeWheel Add() fires two callbacks scheduled at different times in the correct chronological order
1.3: Tests that TimeWheel Add() fires an earlier callback first when it is added after a later callback was already scheduled
1.4: Tests that TimeWheel Add() fires a near-future callback (around 500ms) without being blocked when a far-future callback (very large duration like 90000 hours) was scheduled first
1.5: Tests that TimeWheel Add() fires the callback when scheduling occurs after the wheel has been idle for a brief period (around 500ms sleep before Add)
2.1: Places the test for single callback scheduling in internal/target/queue/timewheel_test.go
2.2: Places the test for two callbacks ordering in internal/target/queue/timewheel_test.go
2.3: Places the test for earlier callback preemption in internal/target/queue/timewheel_test.go
2.4: Places the test for far-future callback not blocking near-future in internal/target/queue/timewheel_test.go
2.5: Places the test for scheduling after idle period in internal/target/queue/timewheel_test.go
3.1: All tests use Go testing framework with *testing.T parameter and t.Errorf for assertions
3.2: All tests follow the naming pattern Test<Subject> or Test<Subject>_<Scenario> starting with uppercase Test prefix
3.3: All tests use defer statement to call Close() on TimeWheel for cleanup
4.1: Uses channel-based callback verification pattern with make(chan TimeSlot) to receive async callback results
